item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you click "+ Options" at the bottom of the page and then click "Page Source", you can scroll down and find the code the author used for this SCP:

 [[span style="background-color: black; color: black"]] [INSERT TEXT HERE] [[/span]]

which will return [INSERT TEXT HERE].

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just wondering, why did you make the blacked-out bits have actual words in them? Could've
used space to achieve the same effect I believe. Quite a funny skip anywise

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I’ll be honest, I expected this to go in a completely different direction than it actually did. I think it worked out alright, considering that that route has already been taken by SCP-3027.

All in all, it’s a pretty well-executed skip. The concept is definitely original, and the execution is good to match it. If there’s any room for improvement, though, I’d say that it’s in the third interview. All it really says is “this joke came out of nowhere!”, and I see nothing that could be lost if either A). it was cut completely or B). it was reworked but with a different motive.

+ Some Nitpicking
- Some Nitpicking

Under no circumstances is SCP-3369 to be transcribed into digital format except on an air-gapped computer during testing

This makes it sound like there’s only one air-gapped computer here to test with. Maybe make “computer” plural instead?

Personnel found attempting unauthorized spread of SCP-3369 and SCP-3369-1 are to be disciplined, amnesticized, and - when applicable - transferred away from Site-28.

I feel like these dashes should be replaced with commas.

All in all, a well-written, well thought-out skip. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thankya kindly, glad you enjoyed it!

And ayup, that's a pretty slick effect, innit? I stole it from another article, and if you want to steal it (or any other interesting piece of code) yourself, just scroll down to the bottom of the article, hit +Options, then Page Source. You'll be looking for the code that includes "span". What it does is change the color of the text and the background, with this specific configuration making both of 'em black. Use it in good health! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good SCP, boys. I liked a lot the idea of giving conciousness to a joke, and it's cute how he asks if it is funny or not, hahaha.

I have a question that I really need you to answer. How did you made that "uncensoring effect" with the blackboxes? I really would like to use that effect in my future SCP articles.

Thank you, and keep on writing as well as here!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really original. Gotta love an article that makes you feel for something that only exists in people's heads.

item.title: Co-Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Roll credits!

Aleph-Null - Story Concept and Structure
Jim North - Story Editing and Expansion

Thank you, thank you, you've been a wonderful audience! Remember to tip your waitress!

Want an encore performance? Check out my author's page!
